steps.title: What meaning does the blank need?
steps.content: Drivers who think the toll is too high are "unlikely to be ______ a proposal to increase the toll." They oppose higher tolls, so the blank must mean something like open to or in favor of — because they are unlikely to be that.

steps.title: Substitute each choice
steps.content:
The [phrase] needs to mean favorably disposed toward, so its negation makes sense. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — receptive to: open and willing to consider — drivers who think the toll is too high are unlikely to be receptive to raising it further. ✓
  • Choice B — apprised of: informed about — drivers are already aware of the proposal; being "unlikely to be informed" doesn't match the argument about persuading people. ✗
  • Choice C — incensed by: made angry by — drivers opposed to higher tolls would actually be incensed (angry), so saying they are "unlikely to be incensed" contradicts the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — cited in: referenced or quoted in — being cited in a proposal is irrelevant to whether they support it. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
